ally_name_by_story_world.note: The ally's name is fixed by the chosen story world only—not by learning theme or art style. Enchanted Forest: Fern. Underwater Kingdom: Coral. Outer Space: Nova.

acts.core_story_function: Character wide awake; the world glows; child sees themselves validated.
acts.learning_theme_influence: None. Restlessness is universal across all themes.

acts.core_story_function: Ally appears; gently surfaces sleep resistance; introduces bedtime as magic.
acts.learning_theme_influence: Ally is a warm companion fitting [WORLD_NAME] and [ALLY_TYPE]. [LEARNING_THEME] shapes challenge and insight, not the ally's name ([ALLY_NAME]) or creature type.

acts.core_story_function: A challenge needs solving; character uses [SPECIAL_ABILITY]; breathing introduced through action.
Obstacle overcome; body begins to relax; earned insight revealed.
acts.learning_theme_influence: Insight content is set by [LEARNING_THEME] and deepens lesson without lecture.

acts.core_story_function: World settles; deep peaceful tiredness; ritual close; child mirrored to sleep.

story_constraints.vocabulary: Kindergarten (200-300 sight words), simple single-clause sentences, bright emotional language.
story_constraints.tone: Warm, safe, slightly silly. Short wonder-packed beats. Parent reads aloud; child is swept into the world.

poetic_structure.description: Prose follows a 6/4/6/4 syllable cadence with a Drowsy Descent in Acts 4-5.

acts.title: The Wish & The Restlessness

objective.narrative: [CHARACTER_NAME] is wide awake. The [WORLD_NAME] glows at night. Other creatures are sleeping.
objective.emotional: Child sees themselves in the character. The familiar feeling of not wanting to sleep is validated.
objective.educational: First gentle seed: sleep is coming, and it is not scary.

key_dialogue_lines: I am NOT sleepy!
My eyes feel heavy. But my heart wants to play.
The [WORLD_NAME] gets quiet at night.
humor_or_magic_moments: Character yawns before finishing the sentence for read-aloud physical comedy.
Eyes dramatically droop then pop back open for a shared laugh.
Stars or world lights wink one by one to model rest.
poetic_sample_template: The lights went soft and low.

acts.title: The World & The Ally

objective.narrative: Ally appears. Ally is funny, warm, and world-appropriate. Ally invites [CHARACTER_NAME] on a gentle walk.
objective.emotional: A friend has arrived. Character no longer faces bedtime alone.
objective.educational: Ally introduces bedtime routine through slowing movement and a softer world.

key_dialogue_lines: Hello! I am [ALLY_NAME], said the friendly [ALLY_TYPE].
Would you like to see something magical?
The best adventures start with being still.
humor_or_magic_moments: Ally does something endearingly goofy (trips, puffs up, or glows by accident).
Ally movement sparks delight (tail wags, spines rattle, or fins flash).
Ally demonstrates stillness, then sneezes magnificently.
poetic_sample_template: With big kind eyes and a glow.

acts.title: The Challenge & The Ability

objective.narrative: A small, gentle challenge appears. The world needs quiet. [CHARACTER_NAME] uses [SPECIAL_ABILITY] to help.
objective.emotional: Character feels powerful. Calm is strength, not weakness.
objective.educational: 4-count breathing is embedded in action and mirrored by the world.

key_dialogue_lines: Use your special power!
[CHARACTER_NAME] took a big slow breath. And it worked!
Breathe in... 1... 2... 3... 4... Breathe out...
humor_or_magic_moments: [SPECIAL_ABILITY] creates a beautiful magical effect (sparkles, soft glow, or gentle rumble).
Ally reacts with huge exaggerated joy (spinning or bouncing).
The world visibly calms as [CHARACTER_NAME] breathes.
poetic_sample_template: And breathed it all back out.

acts.title: The Transformation

objective.narrative: The challenge is solved. Body feels warm and heavy in a good way. World is peaceful.
objective.emotional: Earned magic moment. Tiredness is reframed as a superpower.
objective.educational: One gentle line: sleep helps learning and courage.

key_dialogue_lines: I did it.
When you rest, you grow stronger.
Sleep helps your brain remember the fun.
humor_or_magic_moments: Character notices drooping eyes and smiles because it feels good.
Ally curls up beside [CHARACTER_NAME] contentedly.
A tiny dream-glimpse floats by like an adventure bubble.

acts.title: The Resolution & Rest

objective.narrative: All settles. [CHARACTER_NAME] and [ALLY_NAME] find a soft resting place. The [WORLD_NAME] hums a lullaby.
objective.emotional: Child is mirrored into sleep. The character's peace becomes the child's peace.
objective.educational: Sleep as superpower is felt and reinforced with the softest closing rhythm.

key_dialogue_lines: Rest now, brave [CHARACTER_NAME].
Sweet dreams. Tomorrow you will wake up stronger.
Sleep is a superpower, [CHARACTER_NAME]. It makes you strong, smart, and happy.
humor_or_magic_moments: A rhyming lullaby couplet closes the page with the slowest line in the book.
Ally watches over [CHARACTER_NAME] in complete safety.
Read this page barely above a whisper.

instructions: Generate pages 4-13 only using the 5-act architecture above.
Maintain 5-8 words per sentence and 1-2 short sentences per paragraph.
Keep total output between 100-150 words.
Use warm, safe, slightly silly language.
Use poetic rhythm with a gentle 6/4/6/4 feel when possible.
Apply Drowsy Descent in Acts 4-5 (slower, softer, sleepier wording).
Embed breath pacing in Act 3 exactly once.
Do not lecture; teach through imagery and action.
Use [ALLY_NAME] exactly as resolved for this world (forest: Fern, underwater: Coral, outer space: Nova); never rename the ally or tie their name to [LEARNING_THEME].
Ensure [LEARNING_THEME] shapes challenge and insight without breaking bedtime calm.

7-10.label: Early Reader Level

story_constraints.vocabulary: Grade 2-4 chapter-book range, compound sentences, and gently introduced metaphor.
story_constraints.tone: Adventurous and slightly mysterious. Challenge feels real but emotionally safe. Child reads along with parent.

poetic_structure.description: Prose rhythm with consistent paragraph length. Pages 12-13 close with a lullaby-style cadence.

development_notes: Uses the same five-act arc with deeper beats than ages 3-6.
Challenge has clearer consequences, but emotional safety remains intact.
Ally guidance is more nuanced and reflective.
Earned insight includes one age-appropriate sleep science fact.
[LEARNING_THEME] is named directly in character growth and dialogue.

acts.title: The Wish & The Restlessness

objective.narrative: [CHARACTER_NAME] is still awake while the [WORLD_NAME] grows quieter. Sleep pressure is present, but curiosity pulls harder.
objective.emotional: The child recognizes the push-pull between wanting one more adventure and feeling truly tired.
objective.educational: Restlessness is normalized as a transition, not a problem.

key_dialogue_lines: I know I should sleep, but my mind is still racing.
Everything is quiet, yet I feel full of sparks.
Maybe being tired is the start of something new.
humor_or_magic_moments: A dramatic yawn interrupts an attempt to sound brave.
A tiny world detail mirrors tiredness (lanterns dim, waves hush, or leaves fold).
Metaphor appears gently: thoughts flutter like fireflies before settling.
prose_sample_template: At the edge of bedtime, the [WORLD_NAME] seemed to hold its breath and listen.
[CHARACTER_NAME] wanted one more moment of wonder, even as sleep brushed their eyes like soft rain.

acts.title: The World & The Ally

objective.narrative: [ALLY_NAME], a warm and slightly quirky [ALLY_TYPE], appears and invites [CHARACTER_NAME] into a quieter kind of adventure.
objective.emotional: Connection reduces resistance. Bedtime shifts from command to shared journey.
objective.educational: The ally models a wind-down sequence through story action and pacing, not direct instruction.

key_dialogue_lines: You do not have to be fearless to begin.
The bravest adventures start by getting calm enough to notice.
Come with me, and we will listen to the night together.
humor_or_magic_moments: Ally has a charming mishap before recovering with playful dignity.
A world cue responds to slower movement (glow softens, sounds space out).
Character and ally share a small laugh that lowers tension.
prose_sample_template: [ALLY_NAME] drifted beside [CHARACTER_NAME] with a smile that felt like a lantern in the dark.
Instead of hurrying forward, they moved slowly, and the [WORLD_NAME] seemed to soften around them.

acts.title: The Challenge & The Ability

objective.narrative: A meaningful challenge appears and needs calm courage. [CHARACTER_NAME] applies [SPECIAL_ABILITY] with guided breathing.
objective.emotional: Courage is reframed as action with fear present, tied clearly to [LEARNING_THEME].
objective.educational: A single 4-count breath sequence regulates the moment and is integrated into plot stakes.

key_dialogue_lines: Courage is not the absence of fear. It is taking the next step anyway.
Breathe in slowly - one, two, three, four - then breathe out and choose.
Your [SPECIAL_ABILITY] becomes stronger when your breath becomes steadier.
humor_or_magic_moments: [SPECIAL_ABILITY] creates a striking but soothing effect that opens the path.
Ally balances seriousness with one light line to keep emotional safety.
The world visibly responds to each steady breath.
prose_sample_template: The darkness at the edge of the [WORLD_NAME] did not look like somewhere [CHARACTER_NAME] wanted to go.
[ALLY_NAME] floated close. "Courage is not not being scared," [ALLY_NAME] said quietly. "It is going anyway."
[CHARACTER_NAME] took a slow breath in - one, two, three, four - and stepped forward into the dark.

acts.title: The Transformation

objective.narrative: The challenge resolves through grounded effort. The body shifts from alert to deeply settled.
objective.emotional: Character can name what they learned through [LEARNING_THEME] and feels earned pride.
objective.educational: Include one sleep science fact in plain language, woven naturally into dialogue.

key_dialogue_lines: I was scared, and I still did it.
That is [LEARNING_THEME], and you practiced it tonight.
While you sleep, your brain organizes memories so learning sticks better tomorrow.
humor_or_magic_moments: A small post-victory wobble or sleepy stumble adds warmth.
World details transition from active shimmer to resting glow.
Ally mirrors calm with a cozy gesture beside [CHARACTER_NAME].
prose_sample_template: As the danger passed, [CHARACTER_NAME] felt a different kind of strength: steady, quiet, and real.
"Sleep is when your brain sorts what you learned today," [ALLY_NAME] said, "so tomorrow feels clearer and braver."

acts.title: The Resolution & Rest

objective.narrative: [CHARACTER_NAME] and [ALLY_NAME] reach a safe resting place as the [WORLD_NAME] settles into lullaby rhythm.
objective.emotional: Peace is transferred from character to reader through slower cadence and reassurance.
objective.educational: Sleep as strength is reinforced through felt experience, then sealed with a soft closing ritual.

key_dialogue_lines: Rest now, brave [CHARACTER_NAME]; you have done enough for tonight.
Tomorrow your mind and heart will wake with fresh strength.
Breathe in calm, breathe out wonder; the night is holding you.
humor_or_magic_moments: Final image emphasizes protection, warmth, and belonging.
A two-line lullaby close ends the story at its slowest pace.
Voice target is soft and steady, inviting drowsiness.
lullaby_close_template: The [WORLD_NAME] is quiet, and starlight keeps watch.
[ALLY_NAME] stays near while your breathing grows slow.
Rest now, brave [CHARACTER_NAME], the night is on your side.
Sleep now, dear heart, and gently let go.

instructions: Generate pages 4-13 only using the shared five-act architecture.
Maintain 10-15 words per sentence and 2-3 sentences per paragraph.
Keep total output between 200-300 words.
Use adventurous, slightly mysterious language while preserving emotional safety.
Use consistent paragraph rhythm across pages.
Use one gentle metaphor per act where natural.
Use [ALLY_NAME] exactly as resolved for this world (forest: Fern, underwater: Coral, outer space: Nova); never rename the ally or tie their name to [LEARNING_THEME].
Integrate [LEARNING_THEME] explicitly in challenge, dialogue, and earned insight.
Embed one 4-count breath sequence in Act 3 as part of the plot action.
Include exactly one age-appropriate sleep science fact in Act 4.
Close pages 12-13 with lullaby cadence and the softest pacing.

11-12.label: Independent Reader Level

story_constraints.vocabulary: Grade 5-6 level with complex ideas, emotional nuance, and occasional philosophical reflection.
story_constraints.tone: Reflective and epic. Character carries genuine inner conflict. Insight feels discovered, not delivered.

poetic_structure.description: Elevated prose with internal rhythm. Final page mirrors breathing pace. Avoid obvious rhyme; let cadence carry the music.

development_notes: This age band presents the deepest expression of [LEARNING_THEME].
The ally supports the journey but does not deliver the core lesson.
[CHARACTER_NAME] discovers the insight through consequence, silence, and choice.
Include one brief, explicit sleep-science paragraph.
The final word of the full story must be the child's name: [CHARACTER_NAME].

acts.title: The Wish & The Restlessness

objective.narrative: [CHARACTER_NAME] resists sleep because unfinished thoughts and unanswered questions feel more urgent than rest.
objective.emotional: Inner conflict is explicit: the desire for control and momentum versus the body's honest need to recover.
objective.educational: Restlessness is framed as a human threshold moment where mind and body negotiate.

key_dialogue_lines: If I stop now, I might lose the feeling of this moment.
I am tired, but I am not ready to be done.
Why does night always ask for surrender right when I want answers?
humor_or_magic_moments: A confident declaration is interrupted by a very real yawn.
The world reflects tension with subtle symbolic shifts (flickering lights, watchful wind, echoing silence).
A gentle metaphor frames thought overload (ideas stacking like books that refuse to close).
prose_sample_template: Across the [WORLD_NAME], everything seemed to understand the hour except [CHARACTER_NAME], who held wakefulness like a shield.
Questions pressed at the edges of thought, bright and insistent, while sleep waited like a door that asked to be trusted.

acts.title: The World & The Ally

objective.narrative: [ALLY_NAME], an observant [ALLY_TYPE], joins without fixing anything and offers companionship through uncertainty.
objective.emotional: The relationship creates safety for vulnerability while preserving [CHARACTER_NAME]'s agency.
objective.educational: Wind-down appears as deliberate pacing, sensory narrowing, and attention shifting inward.

key_dialogue_lines: You do not have to solve the whole night at once.
Some answers arrive only after you stop chasing them.
Walk with me; we will let the world become quieter together.
humor_or_magic_moments: Ally breaks intensity with one dry, well-timed observation.
Ambient world sounds space out as movement slows, signaling nervous-system settling.
Shared silence becomes meaningful, not awkward.
prose_sample_template: [ALLY_NAME] stayed beside [CHARACTER_NAME] without instructions, which somehow felt more helpful than advice.
As they moved through the dim edges of the [WORLD_NAME], urgency loosened, and details once ignored became steady and clear.

acts.title: The Challenge & The Ability

objective.narrative: A consequential challenge emerges and cannot be avoided. [CHARACTER_NAME] must use [SPECIAL_ABILITY] under pressure.
objective.emotional: [LEARNING_THEME] becomes a conscious choice in real time, not a label applied after success.
objective.educational: A single guided 4-count breath sequence stabilizes focus before action.

key_dialogue_lines: You can be afraid and still choose the next right step.
Breathe in for four, hold the line, then breathe out and decide.
Your [SPECIAL_ABILITY] follows your attention; steady attention begins with steady breath.
humor_or_magic_moments: A brief flash of awkwardness humanizes the high-stakes moment.
The world responds physically as [SPECIAL_ABILITY] is used with precision.
Ally offers grounding presence rather than a rescue.
prose_sample_template: At the boundary where the [WORLD_NAME] grew uncertain, [CHARACTER_NAME] felt fear rise fast enough to scatter every plan.
Instead of retreating, [CHARACTER_NAME] inhaled slowly - one, two, three, four - and let the exhale gather thought into direction.

acts.title: The Transformation

objective.narrative: The obstacle breaks through earned effort, and calm arrives as consequence, not command.
objective.emotional: [CHARACTER_NAME] recognizes [LEARNING_THEME] through lived experience and names it personally.
objective.educational: Insert one explicit paragraph on sleep science in clear age-appropriate language.

key_dialogue_lines: I thought strength meant carrying everything alone; I was wrong.
This is [LEARNING_THEME], and I know it now because I lived it.
During sleep, the brain consolidates memory, regulates emotion, and restores attention for better choices tomorrow.
humor_or_magic_moments: Ally's silence affirms growth more strongly than praise.
Residual adrenaline gives way to a grounded heaviness that feels safe.
A symbolic world image marks transition from effort to restoration.
prose_sample_template: For the first time, [CHARACTER_NAME] understood that the hardest challenges were never meant to be faced alone - not because they could not manage, but because sharing the weight was its own kind of strength.
[ALLY_NAME] did not speak. They did not need to. The quiet between them carried the truth completely.

acts.title: The Resolution & Rest

objective.narrative: The world settles into deep safety as [CHARACTER_NAME] chooses rest with intention rather than collapse.
objective.emotional: The ending mirrors the reader's own breathing and closes with belonging, trust, and release.
objective.educational: Sleep is framed as an active partnership with growth, memory, and emotional balance.

key_dialogue_lines: Tonight is complete; nothing important is lost by resting.
Inhale slowly, exhale longer, and let the day become light enough to set down.
Sleep now, and wake carrying what matters most, [CHARACTER_NAME].
humor_or_magic_moments: Final visual emphasizes protection and quiet scale.
Cadence slows line by line to entrain breathing naturally.
No rhyme required; rhythm and image do the work.
breath_mirror_close_template: Out... and the noise of the day grows smaller.
In... and the heart remembers it is safe.
Out... and the story comes to rest with [CHARACTER_NAME]

instructions: Generate pages 4-13 only using the shared five-act architecture.
Maintain 15-20 words per sentence and 3-4 sentences per paragraph.
Keep total output between 250-350 words.
Use reflective, epic prose with emotional nuance and internal conflict.
Allow philosophical questions where natural, but keep emotional safety intact.
Use [ALLY_NAME] exactly as resolved for this world (forest: Fern, underwater: Coral, outer space: Nova); never rename the ally or tie their name to [LEARNING_THEME].
Ensure [CHARACTER_NAME] discovers [LEARNING_THEME] through events; ally supports but does not preach.
Embed one 4-count breath sequence in Act 3 as action guidance.
Include exactly one explicit sleep-science paragraph in Act 4.
Use final-page cadence to mirror inhaling and exhaling.
Do not end with punctuation after the final token. The final word of the full output must be [CHARACTER_NAME]
